header.homeConfirm: Returning to the library leaves the current story; unsaved progress is dropped from this view. Go back?

footer.placeholder: type an action, or / for commands

options.hint: Press {{keys}} or click to choose · use the pencil to edit before sending

mode.label: Settings complexity
mode.preferenceDescription: Simple keeps just what you need to start reading. Advanced adds every service, model, and behind-the-scenes control.

story.title: Defaults are enough
story.description: Story behavior and user memory already have defaults, so you can start without configuring them.
story.body: Switch to Advanced when you want to fine-tune the narrator's long-term taste, what happens behind the scenes, how stories begin, or how choices are suggested.

ai.title: Models and AI services
ai.description: Simple sets up just the text model. Advanced adds search, images, speech, and finer control over which model handles what.

story.title: Narrative behavior and user memory
story.description: Set how new stories begin, what happens behind the scenes, and the preferences the narrator keeps in mind.

presentation.title: Reading presentation
presentation.description: Only reading UI lives here: language, font, size, reveal speed, and background art display.

system.simpleDescription: Choose how much of Settings is visible. Keep the default low-friction, or switch to Advanced for full control.
system.title: Runtime status and environment
system.description: Inspect service status and configure work directories, proxies, and runtime environment values.

llm.title: Text model providers
llm.description: Choose the LLM provider, save API keys, and configure the base endpoint and default models.

search.title: Search providers
search.description: Choose the web search provider and configure its API key; search is a peer capability to text models.

routing.title: Models and call routing
routing.description: Send each kind of request — narration, suggested choices, summaries, and behind-the-scenes helpers — to whichever provider and model you like.

agents.title: Agent capability
agents.description: Set each resident Agent's model, tool permissions, budget, and concurrency limits.

modelCatalog.title: Model catalog and defaults
modelCatalog.description: Add extra models exposed by providers, then tune the small/large foreground/background defaults if needed.

narrativeCalls.title: Narrative calls
narrativeCalls.description: Choose the models for narration, the choices readers see, and quick background helpers.

maintenanceCalls.title: Maintenance calls
maintenanceCalls.description: Pick the models for behind-the-scenes upkeep — story-keeping, memory, summaries, and reading web pages.

subagentClasses.title: Subagent classes
subagentClasses.description: Set defaults for the helper agents a task can call on: general, continuity, research, and planning.

residentAgents.title: Resident Agents
residentAgents.description: Tune each always-available Agent's model, tools, budget, temperature, and concurrency.

behavior.title: Story behavior
behavior.description: Set how new stories begin, how the reader interacts, and how the behind-the-scenes story team runs.

memory.title: Memory and preferences
memory.description: Inspect model-maintained story memory, edit your reading preferences, and control cross-story memory.

display.title: Reading display
display.description: Configure the reading interface language, layout, text reveal, text markings, and visual layers.

storyMedia.title: Story media and modes
storyMedia.description: Control whether stories can output rich blocks, media, illustrations, scene backdrops, and comic mode; image credentials still live under AI → Image.

image.title: Story illustrations
image.description: Set up how your story creates its own pictures.

tts.title: Read aloud
tts.description: Set up the read-aloud voice and speed.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: Choose how much of Settings is visible. Simple is for daily use; Advanced exposes the full control surface.

status.title: Service status
status.description: See what's connected and working right now.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Configure work directories, HTTP proxy, and runtime environment.

customEndpoint.deleteConfirm: Delete this endpoint and its saved key?

tts.customHint: POSTs {baseUrl}/audio/speech with response_format=pcm, one sentence per request. Sentence-synced reading keeps working.

enable.label: Read the story aloud (Doubao / Volcano)
enable.description: Reads the story aloud as it's written, with the words appearing in time with the voice.

tts.voiceHint: Enter the voice you enabled in your Volcano console (large-model voices usually end in _bigtts).

apikeys.pickHint: Pick a provider above to set the API key and test the connection.
apikeys.shadowHint: These values come from a higher-priority settings layer and win over this page. Edit or remove:

custom.title: Custom providers
custom.intro: Define any number of OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic-format endpoints. Each becomes a provider choice in Model routing and Agents, and can be set as the default here.
custom.name: Name
custom.aliasHint: auto-generated from the name when empty
custom.thinkingHelp: Follow per-call keeps narration fast (no thinking) and lets agents think — sends { thinking: { type } } like Kimi/MiMo. Use Passthrough only if the endpoint 400s on that field.

routing.title: Assign models by call
routing.description: Keep ordinary setup on Providers; use this advanced page only for routing. Provider, model, and parameter changes save automatically and apply to the next model request.
routing.catalogDescription: {{builtin}} built-in defaults are already available; add only the extra models exposed by a provider.
routing.label: Label
routing.advancedParametersHint: Run lane, sampling, and timeout controls stay hidden until you need fine control.
routing.noCustomModels: No custom models. Built-in defaults are already available in route selectors.
routing.noMatches: No matching calls in this group.
routing.savedRoute: saved · next call uses this route

agents.title: Assign capability by Agent
agents.description: Each resident Agent can have its own model, tool permissions, and work budget. The list shows effective values first; expand only when you need to tune.
agents.advancedModelHint: The run lane is usually automatic; adjust only when changing foreground/background routing.
agents.saved: saved · next agent run uses this config

image.intro: Set up image generation so your story can create its own pictures. Even without this it can still find images online; creating new ones needs a provider, API key, and model.
image.notConfigured: base URL + API key + model required
image.costWarning: Warning: testing sends a real image generation request and may consume paid provider credits.
image.costConfirm: Run a real image generation test now? This request may incur provider charges.

music.intro: Connect NetEase Cloud Music so your story can play music that fits each scene. You'll need a developer app from NetEase — enter its details below and sign in, then turn on background music under Behavior.
music.leaveBlank: Leave blank to keep the saved one
music.deviceHint: Most setups can leave this empty — fill it only if your NetEase app needs specific device details.
music.accessTokenPlaceholder: Paste your access token to sign in
music.ready: Connected — music is ready to play.
music.needMembership: Connected, but this song wouldn't play — your account may need a membership.
music.signInToPlay: Connected. Sign in to play music.

qr.scan: Scan this with the NetEase Cloud Music app

display.narrationSpeedHint: How fast the words appear as you read. Takes effect right away.
display.autoScrollOff: Don't auto-scroll while the story streams
display.backgroundArt: Show background art when a scene has a cover image
display.sceneBackdrop: Show the scene backdrop image behind the story
display.customRichBlocks: Let story cards use their own style
display.layoutScaleHint: Proportionally widens the reading column and library covers — for large displays.

preferences.label: User memory
preferences.hint: This is what the narrator reads when shaping voice and style. Edit freely — plain markdown, one fact per line is fine.
preferences.confirmReset: Reset reader preferences? This clears the global USER.md preferences and cannot be undone.

memory.viewerHintSimple: Current story memory stays inside this novel; cross-story memory can be switched off above.
memory.viewerHintAdvanced: Inspect the memory files the model reads or maintains.
memory.crossStoryDisabled: Cross-story memory is off. Existing notes are visible here but are not injected into model context and will not be updated.

crossStory.label: Cross-story Memory
crossStory.description: When on, model-observed reader habits and reusable references can carry across novels. Turn it off to keep model memory story-local.

story.label: Current Story Memory
story.empty:
# Story Memory

(No long-term memory for this story yet.)

user.label: Reading Preferences
user.empty:
# User Memory

(No saved reading preferences yet.)

storyStart.title: Opening and setup
storyStart.description: Controls how deeply a new story prepares before the first scene, and whether you preview the narrator voice first.

interaction.title: Reader interaction
interaction.description: Controls whether each turn ends with suggested actions the reader can pick directly.

engine.title: Behind-the-scenes story team
engine.description: Controls who maintains the world, characters, pacing, and files in the background, plus which local tools they can use.

diagnostics.title: Testing and recording
diagnostics.description: For debugging, comparison runs, and raw model-call capture; everyday reading usually does not need these.

richStory.title: Rich blocks and story assets
richStory.description: Controls whether stories can output structured cards and reference media placed in the story's media folder.

storyMedia.title: Illustrations, backdrops, and comic mode
storyMedia.description: Controls whether pictures, scene backdrops, and comic mode can become part of the narrative output.

optionsEnabled.label: Suggest choices each turn
optionsEnabled.description: After each turn, offer a few numbered choices you can pick instead of typing.

displayPacing.label: Reveal text at a reading pace
displayPacing.description: Show the words at a calm, steady pace. Turn off to show them the moment they arrive.

residentTeam.label: Story team
residentTeam.description: Behind the scenes, a small team keeps track of your world, pacing, characters, and memory. On by default — turn off to use a single, lighter-weight helper.

crossStoryMemory.label: Cross-story Memory
crossStoryMemory.description: Let openovel carry model-observed reader habits and reusable references into other novels. Turn off to keep model memory story-local; existing cross-story memory remains until you reset it.

formatContract.label: Rich rendering
formatContract.description: Let your story show more than plain text — status bars, stat panels, and terminal-style blocks — when it fits the scene.

storyIncludes.label: Media in stories
storyIncludes.description: Let stories show images, video, audio, or text you place in their media folder. Needs Rich rendering on.

imageGen.label: Story illustrations
imageGen.description: Let your story show pictures it finds or creates as the plot moves forward. Turns on Rich rendering and media too — set it up under Settings → Image.

imageBackground.label: Scene backgrounds
imageBackground.description: Let prepared scene images appear as a soft, dimmed backdrop behind the story text for extra atmosphere. The app keeps the text fully readable with a built-in veil. Turns on Rich rendering and media too; pairs best with Story illustrations.

characterSheets.label: Character sheets (experimental)
characterSheets.description: Have the image helper keep a visual reference for each major character from the character cards — a written look spec plus a generated reference sheet — and hold every later illustration to it. Needs Story illustrations on.

comicMode.label: Comic mode (experimental)
comicMode.description: Adds a per-story switch to the library card menu: a story in comic mode plays as a picture-story strip (generated panels with captions) instead of prose. Set up image generation under Settings → Image first.
comicMode.needsImageProvider: Comic mode generates pictures every turn. Configure image generation under Settings → Image first.

fastMode.label: Fast mode (experimental)
fastMode.description: Adds a per-story switch to the library card menu: a story in fast mode plays in short bursts that move the plot quickly and stop at the next meaningful decision, with the choices carrying the weight.

musicGen.label: Background music (experimental)
musicGen.description: Plays mood music that fits your story. A small bar at the top of the window lets you pause and set the volume. Connect your music account under Settings → Music first.

initNarratorPreview.label: Preview the voice at setup
initNarratorPreview.description: When starting a new story, hear the narrator's voice on a draft and fine-tune it over a round or two before you begin. Adds a little time up front.

bashTool.label: Shell tool for the story team (advanced)
bashTool.description: Let the behind-the-scenes team run shell commands (such as jq) to read and update your world's data. Runs in an OS sandbox: no internet access, and it can only write inside this story's files. Off by default; needs macOS, or Linux with bubblewrap.

disableBackground.label: Pause all background work (testing)
disableBackground.description: Stop everything that happens behind the scenes between turns. For testing and comparisons.

disableStorykeeper.label: Pause story-keeping (testing)
disableStorykeeper.description: Stop the behind-the-scenes helper that keeps track of canon and characters.

disableMemoryReview.label: Pause memory updates (testing)
disableMemoryReview.description: Stop the behind-the-scenes helper that builds up long-term memory.

disableContextInserts.label: Pause auto context cards (testing)
disableContextInserts.description: Stop automatically bringing in the most relevant character and world notes each turn.

recordCalls.label: Record raw model calls (developer)
recordCalls.description: Save every model call — the exact prompt sent and the full reply — to story/packets/calls.jsonl, for building training data. Off by default; produces large files.

onboarding.title: Welcome to openovel
onboarding.apiKeyLead: Pick a provider and paste a key. Change it later from the gear icon.
onboarding.stylePlaceholder: A passage, book, author, or genre you like — or leave empty.

languageOptions.otherPlaceholder: e.g. 日本語 / Español / mostly English with occasional Japanese

preferenceForm.proseReferencePlaceholder: A passage you like, a book, an author, or a genre whose voice the narrator should echo.

initChat.greeting: What kind of story should "{{storyName}}" become? Tell me a few sentences — or paste a full worldbook — and I'll start drafting the canon and characters.
initChat.resumedInterrupted: The previous initialization was interrupted — continuing from where it left off…
initChat.resumeUnfinished: This story's initialization never finished. The draft so far is restored; send the prefilled request to continue building on what was already written.

placeholder.initial: Describe the story you want, or paste a worldbook

initChat.enterInteractiveHint: Lock in the current draft and start reading
initChat.askMultiHint: Select one or more, then confirm.
initChat.askMultiPlaceholder: Add anything else (optional), then confirm — Cmd-Enter

storyNaming.title: Name your new story
storyNaming.hint: Anything goes — letters, digits, CJK, spaces, colons, emoji.

initDepth.title: Choose initialization depth
initDepth.subtitle: Pick once; change later in Settings.

zero.title: Zero init
zero.short: Your brief becomes the opening setup. No agent.
zero.body: Fastest start. No upfront LLM cost. The narrator picks the brief up on the first reader action and improvises from there.

standard.title: Standard init
standard.short: Agent scaffolds the story with file tools.
standard.body: The init agent uses file tools to build foreground guidance, section files, up to 5 character cards, and durable memory from your brief. No web access.

deep.title: Deep init
deep.short: Agent does web research first, then scaffolds.
deep.body: Best for fan-fiction or real-world settings. The agent searches the web for canon worldbuilding, character profiles, and relationships before writing, citing sources in durable memory and context cards. Longer init time (1-3 min).

initDepth.settingHint: Pops up on the first new story if unset. Change here to override for future stories.

stories.title: From your library
stories.hint: ←→↑↓ navigate · Enter to open · double-click to open

menu.exportInitialMissing: This story has no saved initial version (imported or created before this feature).
menu.restartHint: Current progress is saved as a version
menu.restartMissing: No saved opening to restart from

comic.panelFailed: This panel could not be drawn
